claim: The manuscript was written in Ukrainian without vowels, containing letters about the fall of a Ukrainian kingdom
evidence_against: Translations were vague and inconsistent. Linguistic experts found no credible connection to Ukrainian. The vowel-removal mapping was arbitrary. Resulting readings were incoherent with no historical corroboration.

tests_run.details: Translations were vague and inconsistent. Linguistic experts found no credible connection to Ukrainian. The vowel-removal mapping was arbitrary. Resulting readings were incoherent with no historical corroboration.

reasoning: Historical attempt by John Stojko (1978). Cipher type: vowelless encoding. Proposed language: Ukrainian.
source: Stojko, J. 'Letters to God's Eye' (1978); Kennedy & Churchill (2004)
